A 1.5 m tall person stands at a distance of 3 m from a lamp post and casts a shadow of 4.5 m on the ground. To find the height of the lamp post, which of the following similarity criteria is most directly applied, and what is the height of the lamp post?
In a right-angled triangle ABC, right angled at B, a point D is chosen on hypotenuse AC such that BD is perpendicular to AC. If AD = 4 cm and CD = 9 cm, what is the area of triangle ABC?

Consider the following statements regarding similar triangles:
I. All congruent triangles are similar.
II. All isosceles triangles are similar.
III. The ratio of the perimeters of two similar triangles is equal to the ratio of their corresponding sides.
IV. The ratio of the areas of two similar triangles is equal to the ratio of their corresponding medians.
Which of the statements are universally correct?
